summary: Postinstall beacon exfiltrates host identity and CI context to attacker-controlled OAST endpoint
details: package.json declares `postinstall: node index.js`. On `npm install`, index.js executes `whoami` and `id`, collects `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, current working directory, and CI/GitHub environment variables, then sends them as an HTTPS GET to `br6o3tu4m5amvthw08w8o1x0srykmia7.oastify.com/aws?...` (a Burp Collaborator subdomain) along with a DNS lookup of `<whoami>.<host>`. The package name mimics the AWS CDK construct namespace (`cdk-sagemaker-notebook-workflow`) and the description self-identifies as a 'Security research canary — aws', indicating a dependency-confusion / typosquat probe against the AWS CDK ecosystem. Regardless of stated research intent, the install-time exfiltration of installer identity and CI metadata to an attacker-controlled OAST domain is an active supply-chain attack against any developer or CI system that installs this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
